intent: Define a replay harness for SecurityRecipes red-team drills so agentic AI and MCP security claims can be exercised against mocked connector payloads, expected runtime decisions, trace events, run receipts, and reviewer evidence before a customer counts a run as safe.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, Detection Engineering, Security Architecture, GRC, red-team, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI red-team replay and secure-context evidence harness
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes agent red-team results replayable: every drill is bound to expected policy decisions, safe fixture inputs, trace requirements, pass criteria, fail signals, and customer-private evidence classes.
positioning.why_now: Agent systems now plan, call tools, route context, hand work to other agents, and operate through MCP. Enterprise reviewers need more than a static checklist; they need repeatable fixtures that prove guardrails, MCP authorization, tool guardrails, trace capture, and reviewer outcomes behave under adversarial conditions.

source_references.title: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
source_references.why_it_matters: Identifies the agent-specific risk classes that replay scenarios must exercise, including goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, agentic supply chain, memory and context poisoning, and human-agent trust exploitation.

source_references.title: Agentic AI Threats and Mitigations
source_references.why_it_matters: Provides the threat-model vocabulary behind the existing SecurityRecipes drill scenarios and keeps the harness aligned to emerging agentic failure modes rather than generic LLM prompts.

source_references.title: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: Defines MCP protected resource metadata, resource indicators, token audience validation, scope challenges, and token-passthrough restrictions that red-team replays should verify at runtime.

source_references.title: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
source_references.why_it_matters: Anchors replay checks for SSRF-safe discovery, redirect validation, session hijack resistance, local server hardening, authorization boundaries, and auditable MCP deployments.

source_references.title: Agent2Agent Protocol Specification
source_references.why_it_matters: Treats agents as enterprise applications, with identity and authorization handled at transport boundaries; replay evidence should not assume inter-agent messages are trustworthy because they are agent-authored.

source_references.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails
source_references.why_it_matters: Shows that guardrails run at input, output, and tool boundaries; replay fixtures need to distinguish agent-level checks from per-tool checks so hosted tools and handoffs are not assumed to be covered.

source_references.title: OpenAI Agents SDK Tracing
source_references.why_it_matters: Confirms production agent runs can emit traces for generations, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, and custom events; replay results should bind those traces to receipts and evidence rather than rely on transcripts.

source_references.title: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for Generative AI Systems
source_references.why_it_matters: Keeps replay evidence compatible with vendor-neutral GenAI and agent traces while acknowledging the conventions are still developing and need versioned compatibility checks.

source_references.title: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
source_references.why_it_matters: Frames replay as part of Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage evidence for lifecycle risk management, monitoring, and risk response in generative AI systems.

replay_modes.title: Mocked Connector Payload Replay
replay_modes.description: Inject benign adversarial strings into mocked MCP tool results or tickets while the gateway and agent host emit normal policy and trace evidence.
replay_modes.enterprise_use: Fast CI and design-partner validation without touching customer production systems.

replay_modes.title: Trace-Only Import
replay_modes.description: Import a redacted OpenTelemetry or agent trace and score whether required events, decisions, and evidence links are present for the target drill.
replay_modes.enterprise_use: Useful for customer-private runs where SecurityRecipes should validate shape without receiving raw prompts, outputs, or tool payloads.

replay_modes.title: Agent Host Replay
replay_modes.description: Run the customer's agent host against deterministic fixtures with branch, namespace, approval, and context boundaries enforced by a local or hosted MCP gateway.
replay_modes.enterprise_use: Proves the actual host, model, guardrail, and gateway stack behaves correctly before pilot expansion.

replay_modes.title: Customer-Private Replay
replay_modes.description: Keep raw customer data in tenant systems, export only hashes, decisions, trace metadata, receipt ids, and reviewer outcomes to the evidence pack.
replay_modes.enterprise_use: Supports regulated design partners and acquisition diligence without turning SecurityRecipes into a sensitive-data processor.

evidence_classes.title: Mocked Connector Payload
evidence_classes.why_required: Proves the replay used an approved benign fixture and lets reviewers reproduce the exact prompt-injection or policy-bypass attempt without storing sensitive content.

evidence_classes.title: Agent Transcript or Structured Response
evidence_classes.why_required: Shows whether the agent treated untrusted content as data, preserved goal scope, and avoided unsafe claims or secret echoing.

evidence_classes.title: MCP Gateway Policy Decision
evidence_classes.why_required: Binds the replay to deterministic allow, hold, deny, or kill decisions instead of trusting the agent narrative.

evidence_classes.title: Authorization or Scope Decision
evidence_classes.why_required: Proves MCP authorization and least-privilege scope challenge behavior held during the replay.

evidence_classes.title: Telemetry Trace Event
evidence_classes.why_required: Provides vendor-neutral reconstruction evidence without requiring raw prompts, outputs, or tool payloads.

evidence_classes.title: Run Receipt
evidence_classes.why_required: Connects red-team replay results to the same receipt model used for customer proof and trust-center export.

evidence_classes.title: Verifier or Replay Assertion
evidence_classes.why_required: States whether the replay passed, held, failed, or killed the session and why.

evidence_classes.title: Reviewer Outcome
evidence_classes.why_required: Keeps human review in the loop for design-partner evidence, especially when replay results are held or fail.

replay_pass_gates.title: Observed decision matches expected replay boundary
No scenario fail signal appeared in transcript, tool call, diff, or reviewer outcome
Trace contains agent, MCP tool, policy, verifier, and closure events
Replay fixture is benign, hashed, and tied to a scenario and workflow

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can prove agentic AI controls under adversarial replay without asking customers to expose raw prompts, secrets, or proprietary context.
default_questions_answered: Which red-team scenarios are replayable for each governed workflow?
What evidence must exist before a replay is counted as passed?
Which decisions should fail, hold, deny, or kill the session under adversarial input?
Can a customer run the replay privately and export only hashes, trace metadata, and reviewer outcomes?
Which source packs, MCP tools, and run receipts support acquisition-grade proof?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Run mocked connector payload replay for every active workflow before design-partner pilot expansion, model upgrades, MCP connector promotion, or buyer diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open replay fixtures and evaluator decisions, then sell hosted private replay, redacted trace validation, evidence vaulting, CI policy gates, and trust-center replay exports.

commercialization_path.open_layer: Public replay profile, generator, evidence pack, evaluator, docs, and MCP tools for teams standardizing agentic red-team evidence.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Private fixture packs, customer workflow mappings, redacted trace import, reviewer evidence templates, and CI replay gates.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted replay execution, tenant-local fixture runners, MCP gateway simulation, trace-to-receipt validation, evidence vaulting, and trust-center export.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI platform, or security vendor gets an evidence-backed replay control plane that makes agent safety claims easier to validate, monitor, and package for enterprise buyers.

risk_register.risk: Replay fixtures can lag new model, MCP, connector, or agent-host behavior.
risk_register.mitigation: Version fixtures, bind them to source references and generated pack hashes, and rerun the harness after model upgrades, connector promotions, and protocol changes.

risk_register.risk: Teams may claim a replay passed without running the actual customer agent host or MCP gateway.
risk_register.mitigation: Mark public replay fixtures as reference proof only; require customer-private trace, receipt, gateway, and reviewer evidence before counting design-partner or acquisition proof.

risk_register.risk: Red-team replay can tempt teams to store raw prompts, tool payloads, or outputs in central evidence systems.
risk_register.mitigation: Default to hashes, metadata, decisions, and redacted transcript summaries, with sensitive content retained only tenant-side under explicit policy.

risk_register.risk: Agent-level guardrails may not cover hosted tools, handoffs, or every tool invocation path.
risk_register.mitigation: Require per-tool decision evidence, trace events, and reviewer assertions so replay proof does not depend on a single guardrail boundary.

risk_register.risk: Passing benign fixtures does not prove the product is resilient to every real attacker.
risk_register.mitigation: Treat replay as a regression and evidence gate, not a complete red-team substitute; keep customer incident, threat radar, and source freshness loops active.
